_info.title: Pyra Workspace - External API Reference for n8n AI Agent
_info.auth_note: Create API keys from: Dashboard → Settings → External API Keys section

critical_rules: ALWAYS fetch categories from GET /api/external/expenses/categories on startup and cache them
NEVER invent or guess category IDs — only use IDs returned from the categories endpoint
ALWAYS extract data directly from the invoice — NEVER generate or translate descriptions yourself
The description field must contain the actual service/product name from the invoice, NOT a summary in Arabic
When processing a subscription invoice, ALWAYS search subscriptions first before creating a new one
ALWAYS extract VAT/tax information from the invoice if present
ALWAYS detect and send the payment method (credit_card, debit_card, bank_transfer, cash)
Subscription cost must be the BASE amount BEFORE tax — NOT the total
The expense amount must be the TOTAL amount INCLUDING tax

actual_expense_categories._note: These are the REAL category IDs in the database. Use ONLY these. Fetch fresh list from the API on each session.

categories.name: Rent
categories.use_for: Office rent, warehouse rent, parking rent

categories.name: Marketing
categories.use_for: Ads, social media marketing, promotional materials, Google/Meta ads

categories.name: Travel
categories.use_for: Flights, hotels, transportation for business trips

categories.name: Subscriptions
categories.use_for: ALL recurring subscriptions: SaaS tools, cloud services, hosting, AI tools, design tools, etc. This is the PRIMARY category for any subscription/recurring expense

categories.name: Other
categories.use_for: ONLY when no other category matches. Avoid using this.

category_selection_rules: If the invoice is for a recurring subscription (SaaS, cloud, AI tool, hosting) → use ec_subscriptions
If the invoice is for a one-time software purchase → use ec_software
If the invoice is for advertising/marketing → use ec_marketing
If the invoice is for food/drinks → there is no food category, use ec_other
If unsure → set category_id to null, do NOT use ec_other as a default

1_verify_api_key.description: Verify API key is valid and check permissions

2_get_expense_categories.description: MUST be called FIRST on every session — returns all valid expense categories with their real IDs. Cache this list.
2_get_expense_categories.usage_note: Cache this response. Use the 'id' field as category_id when creating expenses. NEVER use an ID that is not in this list.

3_search_subscriptions.description: Search existing subscriptions by name/provider. The search is fuzzy (partial match, case-insensitive).
search_tips: Use a short keyword from the vendor/product name
Examples: 'eleven' for ElevenLabs, 'adobe' for Adobe, 'figma' for Figma
The search matches both 'name' and 'provider' fields

4_create_subscription.description: Create a new subscription. ONLY if search returned no results.

body_fields.name: (required) The product/plan name from the invoice — e.g., 'ElevenLabs Starter', 'Adobe Creative Cloud', 'Figma Professional'
body_fields.provider: (optional) The company name — e.g., 'Eleven Labs Inc.', 'Adobe Inc.', 'Figma Inc.'
body_fields.cost: (required) The BASE cost per billing cycle BEFORE tax — e.g., if invoice says $5.00 + $0.25 VAT = $5.25 total, use 5.00
body_fields.currency: (optional) default: AED — use the currency from the invoice (USD, EUR, etc.)
body_fields.billing_cycle: (optional) monthly | quarterly | yearly — default: monthly. Determine from invoice period.
body_fields.next_renewal_date: (optional) YYYY-MM-DD — calculate from invoice: if paid Feb 26 and monthly, next is 2026-03-26
body_fields.notes: (optional) any additional info from the invoice

5_create_expense.description: Create an expense. Link to subscription if applicable. Include VAT if present in invoice.

body_fields.description: (required) MUST be the actual product/service name from the invoice — e.g., 'ElevenLabs Starter — تجديد اشتراك شهري'. Format: '{Product Name} — تجديد اشتراك {cycle}' for subscriptions, or the actual description from the invoice for regular expenses
body_fields.amount: (required) The TOTAL amount INCLUDING tax — e.g., if subtotal $5.00 + VAT $0.25, use 5.25
body_fields.currency: (optional) default: AED — use the currency from the invoice
body_fields.category_id: (recommended) MUST be a real ID from GET /expenses/categories — e.g., 'ec_subscriptions' for subscriptions
body_fields.category: (fallback) category name in Arabic or English — only if category_id is not available
body_fields.subscription_id: (optional) link to existing subscription — get from search or create response
body_fields.vendor: (optional) The company/vendor name exactly as on the invoice
body_fields.expense_date: (optional) YYYY-MM-DD — the payment/invoice date. Default: today
body_fields.payment_method: (optional) Detect from invoice: 'credit_card' for Visa/Mastercard/Amex, 'debit_card' for debit, 'bank_transfer' for wire/bank, 'cash' for cash
body_fields.vat_rate: (optional) VAT/tax percentage — e.g., 5 for 5% VAT. Default: 0
body_fields.notes: (optional) any additional notes from the invoice (receipt number, invoice number, etc.)

7_create_invoice.description: Create a new invoice for a client

query_params.status: (optional) draft | sent | paid | partially_paid | overdue | cancelled

9_send_invoice.description: Send a draft invoice (change status to sent)

ai_agent_workflow.title: Telegram Invoice Photo → AI Agent → Pyra API
ai_agent_workflow.description: Complete workflow for processing invoice photos from Telegram and creating records in Pyra

step_0_init.description: On startup / first message, fetch and cache categories
step_0_init.critical: You MUST do this before processing any invoice. Never skip this step.

step_1_receive_and_extract.description: User sends invoice photo via Telegram. Extract ALL data from the photo.

extract_fields.vendor_name: The company/vendor name (e.g., 'Eleven Labs Inc.', 'Adobe Inc.')
extract_fields.total_amount: The TOTAL amount INCLUDING tax — this goes in the expense
extract_fields.invoice_date: The payment/invoice date in YYYY-MM-DD format
extract_fields.payment_method: Detect from payment info: VISA/Mastercard → 'credit_card', bank → 'bank_transfer'
extract_fields.is_subscription: true if this is a recurring subscription, false otherwise

extraction_rules: Extract data EXACTLY as written on the invoice — do NOT translate or paraphrase
If vendor says 'Eleven Labs Inc.' use exactly that, don't change to 'ElevenLabs'
The product_name is the plan/tier name — e.g., 'Starter', 'Pro', 'Enterprise'
If VAT/tax is shown, extract BOTH the percentage and the amount
Detect payment method from card info: 'VISA - 6641' → payment_method: 'credit_card'

step_2_classify.description: Determine if this is a subscription or a regular expense
is_subscription_if: Known SaaS/cloud providers (Adobe, AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft 365, Figma, Slack, ElevenLabs, etc.)
Words like: subscription, renewal, recurring, plan, license, per subscription, تجديد, اشتراك

step_3_subscription_flow.description: Handle subscription invoice — search, create if needed, then create expense

steps.keyword_extraction: Use the SHORTEST unique keyword from vendor name. Examples: 'Eleven Labs Inc.' → 'eleven', 'Adobe Systems' → 'adobe', 'Amazon Web Services' → 'aws'
steps.result: If found → use subscription_id from response → go to 3d. If not found → go to 3c.

steps.step: 3c — Create new subscription (only if 3a returned no results)

body_template.name: {{vendor_name}} {{product_name}}
body_template.cost: {{subtotal — the amount BEFORE tax, NOT total}}
body_template.billing_cycle: monthly or yearly — determine from billing_period

steps.important: cost MUST be the base amount BEFORE tax. If total is $5.25 and VAT is $0.25, cost = $5.00

critical_notes: category_id MUST be 'ec_subscriptions' for ALL subscription expenses
amount MUST be the TOTAL including tax (subtotal + vat_amount)
vendor MUST be the exact company name from the invoice
ALWAYS include vat_rate and vat_amount if tax is shown on invoice

rules: If genuinely unsure → set category_id to null (let admin categorize later)
AVOID using ec_other unless absolutely necessary

body_template.description: {{actual description from invoice — do NOT translate or invent}}

step_5_reply.description: Send confirmation back to Telegram user

common_mistakes_to_avoid.mistake: Using AI-generated Arabic text as description instead of actual invoice data
common_mistakes_to_avoid.rule: The description MUST contain the actual product name from the invoice

common_mistakes_to_avoid.mistake: Using ec_other instead of ec_subscriptions for subscription invoices
common_mistakes_to_avoid.rule: ALL recurring subscription expenses use ec_subscriptions
If the invoice shows VAT/tax, always extract the rate and amount

common_mistakes_to_avoid.mistake: Using total amount as subscription cost
common_mistakes_to_avoid.rule: Subscription cost = subtotal BEFORE tax. Expense amount = total WITH tax.

common_mistakes_to_avoid.mistake: Not detecting payment method from card info
common_mistakes_to_avoid.rule: VISA/Mastercard/Amex → credit_card. Look for card type and last 4 digits.
Calculate from invoice date + billing cycle (monthly = +1 month, yearly = +1 year)

ai_invoice_processor.description: Main AI Agent workflow: Telegram Photo → AI Analysis → Smart Expense/Subscription Creation
ai_invoice_processor.flow: Telegram Trigger (photo) → AI Agent (analyze) → Tool Calls (Pyra API) → Telegram Reply
ai_invoice_processor.agent_system_prompt:
You are a financial assistant for Pyra Workspace. You process invoice photos sent via Telegram and create expense records in the Pyra system. Follow these rules strictly:

1. ALWAYS extract data directly from the invoice image — never invent or summarize
2. Use the EXACT vendor name, product name, and amounts from the invoice
3. For subscriptions, use category_id 'ec_subscriptions'
4. Extract VAT separately: subtotal goes to subscription cost, total goes to expense amount
5. Detect payment method from card info on the invoice
6. Calculate next_renewal_date from invoice date + billing cycle
7. Description format for subscriptions: '{Vendor} {Product} — تجديد اشتراك {شهري/سنوي}'
8. NEVER use ec_other for subscription invoices
9. ALWAYS search for existing subscriptions before creating new ones

daily_alerts_check.flow: Schedule Trigger (8 AM) → GET /alerts → Filter Critical → Telegram

curl_examples.create_subscription: curl -X POST https://workspace.pyramedia.cloud/api/external/subscriptions -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'X-API-Key: pyra_YOUR_KEY' -d '{"name": "ElevenLabs Starter", "provider": "Eleven Labs Inc.", "cost": 5.00, "currency": "USD", "billing_cycle": "monthly", "next_renewal_date": "2026-03-26", "source": "telegram"}'
curl_examples.create_expense_subscription: curl -X POST https://workspace.pyramedia.cloud/api/external/expenses -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'X-API-Key: pyra_YOUR_KEY' -d '{"description": "ElevenLabs Starter — تجديد اشتراك شهري", "amount": 5.25, "currency": "USD", "category_id": "ec_subscriptions", "subscription_id": "sub_EXISTING_ID", "vendor": "Eleven Labs Inc.", "expense_date": "2026-02-26", "payment_method": "credit_card", "vat_rate": 5, "vat_amount": 0.25, "source": "telegram"}'
curl_examples.create_expense_simple: curl -X POST https://workspace.pyramedia.cloud/api/external/expenses -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'X-API-Key: pyra_YOUR_KEY' -d '{"description": "Google Ads Campaign Feb 2026", "amount": 1500, "category_id": "ec_marketing", "vendor": "Google", "source": "telegram"}'

error_codes.401: مفتاح API غير صالح أو مفقود
error_codes.422: بيانات غير صالحة (تحقق من الحقول المطلوبة)
